name: Hershey's 50% Reduced Fat Oatmeal Chip Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Hershey's Reduced Fat Semi-Sweet Baking Chocolate chips
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large bowl, beat together the vegetable oil, granulated sugar, and packed light brown sugar with an electric mixer until well combined.
3. Add the eggs and vanilla extract; beat until creamy.
4. Stir together the flour, baking soda, ground cinnamon, and salt in a separate bowl.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the sugar mixture, mixing well after each addition.
6. Fold in the quick-cooking rolled oats, Hershey's Reduced Fat Semi-Sweet Baking Chocolate chips, and raisins.
7. Drop spoonfuls of dough onto an ungreased cookie sheet.
8. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until golden brown.
9. Cool slightly on the cookie sheet, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
Nutrition facts: Serving size: 1 cookie (26g), Daily Value (%DV): Dietary Fiber 1g (4% DV), Sugars 13g, Protein 2g, Vitamin A (2% DV), Vitamin C (0% DV), Calcium (0% DV), Iron (2% DV). Percent Daily Values are based on a 2,000 calorie diet. Contains 1g of Salatrim per serving, only 55% of which is used by the body. Therefore, this recipe provides 3g of available fat per serving compared to 6g in the regular Oatmeal Butterscotch Cookies recipe. Hershey's is a registered trademark of Hershey Foods Corporation.
